attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

9	#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
10	#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches

23	fi

38	require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"

46	)
47	need_build=0
48	for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
49	  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
50	done
51	if (( need_build )); then
52	  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
53	  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
54	fi
55	
56	# name|command. Roundtrip fixtures share roundtrip_e2e.sh; negative drivers
57	# each have their own harness. Mirrors hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake.
58	TESTS=(
59	  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
60	  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
61	  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
62	  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"

66	  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
67	  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
68	  "rt_shared_mem|bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
69	  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# name|command. Roundtrip fixtures share roundtrip_e2e.sh; negative drivers
# each have their own harness. Mirrors hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake.
TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0 bash $HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem|bash $HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
)

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
pass=0; failed=0; skipped=0
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
for entry in "${TESTS[@]}"; do
  name="${entry%%|*}"
  cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  step "[$name]"
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd"
  rc=$?
  set -e
  case $rc in
    0)  ok   "$name PASS"; pass=$((pass+1)) ;;
    77) warn "$name (rc=77)"; skipped=$((skipped+1)) ;;
    *)  fail "$name (rc=$rc)"; failed=$((failed+1)); FAILED_NAMES+=("$name") ;;
  esac
done

echo
step "summary: $pass passed, $failed failed, $skipped skipped"
if (( failed > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# End-to-end roundtrip:
3	#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state at `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`.
4	#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains at `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, exits 200.
5	#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
6	#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
7	#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
8	#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
9	#
10	# Fixture-parameterized:
11	#   FIXTURE     name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
12	#   EXPECT_RC   expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
13	#
14	# Required environment:
15	#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
16	#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
17	#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
18	#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
19	
20	set -euo pipefail
21	
22	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
23	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
24	
25	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
26	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
27	
28	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
29	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
30	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
31	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
32	
33	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
34	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
35	    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
36	    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
37	  fi
38	done
39	
40	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
41	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
42	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
43	trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
44	
45	echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
46	"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
47	  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
48	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1
49	
50	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
51	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
52	  exit 1
53	fi
54	
55	echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
56	set +e
57	timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
58	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
59	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
60	  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
61	RC=$?
62	set -e
63	
64	if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
65	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
66	  echo "--- sim log ---"
67	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
68	  exit 1
69	fi
70	
71	echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"
72

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state at `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains at `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE     name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC   expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

content.content:
commit 62970b1d5c2ce407a111e33812706655c3ebec09
Author: Nick Hung <nick@andestech.com>
Date:   Wed May 6 11:00:36 2026 +0800

    structural: relocate hybrid contract headers and TCG plugin to hybrid_sim
    
    The handoff ABI headers (state_abi*, insn_match, reg_names) and the QEMU
    TCG plugin both live in the hybrid_sim workspace now, where the
    cross-component contract is owned. vsim becomes a consumer that reaches
    up via ../include/.
    
    No behavior change: same headers, same plugin source, only their
    location in the workspace.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 CMakeLists.txt                          |   2 +-
 cmake/HybridConfig.cmake                |  18 +-
 hybrid/include/hybrid/insn_match.h      |  57 -------
 hybrid/include/hybrid/reg_names.h       |  33 ----
 hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi.h       |  83 ----------
 hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h |  63 -------
 hybrid/qemu_plugin/.gitignore           |   2 -
 hybrid/qemu_plugin/Makefile             |  37 -----
 hybrid/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c     | 283 --------------------------------
 9 files changed, 10 insertions(+), 568 deletions(-)

input.command:
/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64 -M virt -bios none -nographic -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native -kernel hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf -gdb tcp::45678 -S >/tmp/qemu2.log 2>&1 &
QEMU_PID=$!
sleep 1
# probe gdbstub
python3 - <<'PY' 2>&1
import socket, time
s = socket.socket()
s.settimeout(5)
s.connect(('127.0.0.1', 45678))
# Read the initial stop reply (if any)
s.sendall(b'+')
# qSupported
def send(s, body):
    cs = sum(body) & 0xff
    pkt = b'$' + body + b'#' + ('%02x' % cs).encode()
    s.sendall(pkt)
    print("sent", body)
    data = b''
    s.settimeout(3)
    while True:
        try:
            chunk = s.recv(4096)
        except socket.timeout:
            break
        if not chunk: break
        data += chunk
        if b'#' in chunk:
            time.sleep(0.05)
            try:
                chunk2 = s.recv(4096)
                data += chunk2
            except socket.timeout: pass
            break
    print("recv", data[:200])
send(s, b'qSupported:multiprocess+;swbreak+;hwbreak+;qXfer:features:read+')
send(s, b'qXfer:features:read:target.xml:0,100')
PY
kill $QEMU_PID 2>/dev/null
wait $QEMU_PID 2>/dev/null
echo "done"

input.command:
cd /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
./build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64 -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf \
  -plugin qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE_QEMU >/dev/null 2>&1
ls -la $STATE_QEMU
echo "---"
# now run vsim in background and observe
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
timeout 15 hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf \
  --resume-from-qemu $STATE_QEMU \
  --handoff-out $STATE_VSIM \
  --qemu-binary build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64 >$SIM_LOG 2>&1 &
VSIM_PID=$!
sleep 6
echo "=== state of vsim children at 6s ==="
pstree -p $VSIM_PID 2>/dev/null || ps -ef | grep -E "$VSIM_PID|qemu-system" | head -20
echo "=== listener ports ==="
ss -ltn | grep -E "127.0.0.1" | head -10
sleep 4
echo "=== log so far at 10s ==="
cat $SIM_LOG
wait $VSIM_PID 2>/dev/null
RC=$?
echo "=== vsim exit rc=$RC ==="
echo "=== full log ==="
cat $SIM_LOG
rm -f $STATE_QEMU $STATE_VSIM $SIM_LOG

cd /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim
STATE_QEMU=/tmp/h_state_q_$$
STATE_VSIM=/tmp/h_state_v_$$
SIM_LOG=/tmp/h_simlog_$$
./build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64 -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf \
  -plugin qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE_QEMU >/dev/null 2>&1
ls -la $STATE_QEMU
echo "---"
timeout 15 hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf \
  --resume-from-qemu $STATE_QEMU \
  --handoff-out $STATE_VSIM \
  --qemu-binary build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64 >$SIM_LOG 2>&1 &
VSIM_PID=$!
sleep 6
echo "=== children at 6s ==="
ps --ppid $VSIM_PID 2>/dev/null
echo "=== log at 6s ==="
cat $SIM_LOG
wait $VSIM_PID 2>/dev/null
RC=$?
echo "=== vsim rc=$RC ==="
echo "=== final log ==="
cat $SIM_LOG
rm -f $STATE_QEMU $STATE_VSIM $SIM_LOG

content.text:
Cannot reproduce. Ran `FILTER='^handoff_roundtrip$' bash run_e2e.sh` 5x in a row -- all pass with rc=3. Manual repro (drain QEMU 1 -> spawn vsim with `--qemu-binary`) also reaches `[info] hybrid handback: continuing target` and exits 3 cleanly.

The original 124 means `timeout 60` killed vsim. Log stopped after `connecting to gdbstub on tcp::37557` -- that's stderr block-buffered to file, so the actual hang point is later (after restore, while waiting on QEMU 2's semihosting exit). Likely transient: port reuse race, or QEMU 2 wedged.

Re: "turn off the strace" -- did you mean the gdbstub (`-gdb tcp::PORT -S`)? That's the handback contract; vsim spawns QEMU 2 frozen specifically so it can restore GPRs/PC/CSRs over the gdbstub before `c`. Removing `-S` would race the restore against execution. If you want to run QEMU 2 manually, drop `--qemu-binary` from vsim and have it just write `--handoff-out`, then launch QEMU yourself however you want. Want me to add a `--no-handback` (or similar) flag for that?

Or did you mean something else by "strace"?
